name: Swedish Meatballs
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak bread crumbs in milk until softened.
2. Add onion to 3 tablespoons butter in a small saucepan and cook slowly for 3 minutes.
3. Mix the softened bread, cooked onion, and meat together. Add eggs, salt, pepper, nutmeg, and paprika. Mix thoroughly until very smooth and light.
4. Shape the meat into small balls, dusting your hands with flour while shaping the balls.
5. Melt remaining 3 tablespoons butter in a large frying pan. Add meatballs and fry until golden brown all over.
6. Remove from the pan and stir in the concentrated meat extract and 3 tablespoons flour. Stir until well blended.
7. Add water, a few grains of pepper, and cook, stirring constantly until thickened.
8. Reduce heat to low and cook for 5 minutes.
9. Stir in sour cream, a rounded tablespoon at a time, stirring until thoroughly blended after each addition.
10. Return meatballs to the gravy, cover the pan, and simmer very gently for 10 minutes.
11. Serve in a deep casserole. Sprinkle with minced parsley.
Yield: 70 to 80 meat balls.
